description: The Green Gang was a Chinese secret society and criminal triad organization prominent in Shanghai during the early to mid 20th century. It originated from the Luojiao Buddhist sect and grain transport boatmen along the Grand Canal. The gang engaged in criminal, social, and political activities, including opium trafficking. Key leaders included Du Yuesheng and Huang Jinrong.

country_links.evidence_quote: the Green Gang left Shanghai and in the early 1950s opened heroin refineries in Hong Kong.
country_links.context: The Green Gang opened heroin refineries in Hong Kong in the early 1950s.
